hypothesis: If a frozen large language model encoder is combined with a trainable Transformer decoder operating over a discrete vocabulary of material-thickness tokens, then the resulting system (CHROMA) can accurately translate natural language descriptions of structural color into valid multilayer thin-film designs, because the language model's pretrained semantic representations provide sufficient optical-property grounding to enable generalization to novel color targets and linguistic paraphrases as measured by CIE76 ΔE color difference.
